data.title: Microservices
data.description: Amazon employs a microservices architecture, allowing different services to operate independently.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed services communicating through APIs, usually RESTful or gRPC.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: AWS provides a scalable and elastic infrastructure to handle demand fluctuations.
data.technical_spec: Auto-scaling groups, load balancers, and serverless solutions like AWS Lambda.

data.title: Distributed Databases
data.description: Utilizes distributed databases for effective data storage and retrieval.
data.technical_spec: Amazon DynamoDB, RDS and S3 for scalable and reliable data storage.

data.title: Content Delivery Network
data.description: Global CDN ensures low latency and high transfer speeds for content distribution.
data.technical_spec: Amazon CloudFront with edge locations around the globe.

data.title: Service-Oriented Architectures (SOA)
data.description: Loose coupling and high cohesion, which enhances flexibility and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Services published via APIs; uses AWS API Gateway.
